title: Release of the 2025 Bank of Canada FMI Oversight Activities Annual Report
summary: The Bank of Canada published its 2025 Annual Report on FMI (Financial Market Infrastructures) Oversight Activities, reviewing oversight of designated clearing and settlement systems. The report indicates designated FMIs continued modernization initiatives and improved risk management practices in 2025, addressing gaps previously identified by the Bank. The Bank also outlined ongoing and new expectations for FMIs to enhance risk management practices in 2026 and beyond.
key_points: Bank of Canada published 2025 Annual Report on FMI Oversight Activities on March 24, 2026
Report covers oversight of designated clearing and settlement systems (financial market infrastructures)
Designated FMIs continued modernization initiatives throughout 2025
FMIs improved risk management practices, addressing gaps the Bank had previously identified
Bank set ongoing and new expectations for FMIs to enhance risk management in 2026 and beyond
why_it_matters: FMI oversight is part of the market infrastructure and plumbing framework that ensures the integrity of clearing and settlement processes. While this announcement signals continued supervisory attention to systemic risk management practices at Canadian market infrastructures, it contains no immediate market-moving information. The full report would be required for substantive market structure insights.
market_structure_context: This press release announces the publication of an annual report on Financial Market Infrastructure (FMI) oversight. FMI oversight—specifically of clearing and settlement systems—relates to market structure as it covers the plumbing infrastructure that enables securities and payments settlement. The announcement states designated FMIs are continuing modernization initiatives and addressing previously identified risk management gaps, suggesting incremental improvements to market infrastructure resilience rather than structural changes.
